title: citrus × bergamia var. ventricosa
intro: Citrus × bergamia var. ventricosa, commonly known as bergamot, is a variety of citrus plant that is native to the Mediterranean region and is characterized by its distinctive, rounded shape and fragrant peel. The medicinal properties of bergamot include its high concentration of limonene, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of bergamot include reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and aiding in weight loss, due to its ability to improve digestion and boost metabolism. Bergamot is typically used in herbal preparations such as tea, essential oil, and extracts, which can be used to promote relaxation, reduce inflammation, and support overall well-being.
